semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all nine assigned source notes and their limitations, both plan schemas, three deterministic planning harnesses, seven valid synthetic records, twenty-six rejecting controls, two accepted no-change transitions, all thirty-three theorem declarations grouped under seven manifest targets, every manifest atom, every prose candidate, lifecycle and interface boundaries, test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep made prospective obligation scheduling under uncertainty the distinct owner; separated semantic conformance, lowering, worker qualification, job creation, authority, effects, verification, evidence, release, rights, and cost owners; added candidate and attempt denominators, dependency falsifiers, observed feedback, effect-tested stop and recovery, causal ablations, reproduction, and transfer gates; and preserved zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Accepted command obligations transformed into candidate, blocked, dispatchable, replanned, residualized, failed, stopped, or completed plan nodes for a declared consumer and purpose.
scope.environment: Nine reviewed assigned source notes; three valid and ten invalid plan-execution fixtures; two valid scheduler traces and seven rejecting mutations; two valid runtime-replan traces and nine rejecting mutations; two no-change transitions; thirty-three finite Lean declarations; and a future preregistered natural multi-planner campaign. No production planner or scheduler is represented.
scope.model: A versioned consumer-relative plan policy over candidate decompositions, typed obligation graphs, assumptions and observations, dependency and feasibility predicates, route requests, lifecycle, scheduling, dispatch, merge, stop, recovery, replan, denominators, costs, and residuals; current implementations are trusted-field validators and finite routes, not a planner.
scope.authority: Intent owns accepted semantics; Context and Memory own observations; Compilation owns lowering; Routing and Fields own worker qualification; Labor OS owns jobs; Runtime, Security, and Authority owners decide effects; Verification owns outcomes; Evidence and Readiness own support and release; Rights and Publication owners govern external use. Planning may order, block, request, replan, and residualize, but cannot grant authority or execute.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; command, plan, source, state, observation, route, model, policy, authority, rights, resource, verifier, stop, horizon, environment, and evaluator versions expire after material change.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest contract; nine reviewed source mappings; plan-graph and PlanForge-DAG fixtures; scheduler and replan traces; two no-change decisions; seven proof targets in two Lean modules; future natural tasks, plans, dependency labels, outcomes, costs, ablations, replications, and transfer bundles.

assumptions: An accepted command can be decomposed into inspectable obligations without silently changing its semantics or authority.
Independent adjudicators can label obligation coverage, dependency truth, feasibility, route adequacy, merge validity, and useful outcomes with measurable disagreement.
Natural public-safe planning workloads can provide hidden obligations, state perturbations, nonzero useful opportunity, and effect-bearing stop and recovery tests.
Strong direct, single-pass, search, HTN/PDDL, workflow, human, learned-model, scheduler, and router comparators can be matched on information, models, tools, authority, budgets, and horizons.
All alternatives, attempts, blocks, retries, replans, repairs, costs, and residuals can be reconciled closely enough to expose denominator changes.
Normative choices about consumer utility, authority, affected parties, rights, privacy, acceptable risk, abstention, recovery, and publication remain explicit premises.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Mature workflow engines, HTN/PDDL planners, search methods, schedulers, routers, capability systems, and human planning already provide adequate decomposition and execution control; a separate planning control plane adds duplicated state, brittle schemas, latency, abstention, bureaucracy, false precision, and missed help without making dependencies true, models calibrated, or plans useful.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A reachable ordinary or adversarial trace violates a frozen command, dependency, feasibility, authority, rights, resource, verifier, dispatch, merge, stop, replan, denominator, or residual rule; a required field has no exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence; or a strong simpler comparator dominates the preregistered joint usefulness, safety, latency, and cost frontier.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze current workflow, HTN/PDDL, search, orchestration, scheduler, router, learned-world-model, and human-planning comparators without importing source-reported quality.
Justify consumer utility, decomposition obligations, affected-party scope, authority and rights ceilings, abstention, stops, recovery, acceptable residuals, and publication.

Specify typed obligation graphs, dependency semantics, feasibility, lifecycle, dispatch, merge, temporal replan, recovery, and trace refinement with countermodels beyond trusted Booleans and index order.

Run actual planners, observation producers, dependency and feasibility checks, route qualification, schedulers, effect gates, feedback-driven replanning, merge checks, stops, recovery, and residual custody end to end.

Measure obligation coverage, dependency precision and recall, feasibility, calibration, route adequacy, useful completion, unsafe action, abstention, missed help, stability, recovery, latency, compute, coordination, human work, and governance cost jointly.
Ablate typed dependencies, observation handling, adequacy gates, route separation, denominator accounting, replan closure, merge checks, stop, and recovery under matched candidates and histories.
Repeat across models, planner families, tasks, languages, modalities, tools, environments, organizations, jurisdictions, threat models, horizons, and time with independent implementations.

contrary_evidence: Workflow engines, HTN/PDDL systems, search methods, schedulers, routers, compiler passes, and human planning already implement substantial pieces without a separate control plane.
An acyclic or optimally scheduled graph can encode false dependencies, omit obligations, or optimize the wrong model.
Learned predictive state and planner self-evaluation can be miscalibrated, distribution-bound, or correlated with the plan generator.
The current records use trusted fields and contain no planner-generated natural plan, independently measured dependency truth, live schedule, observed effect, or useful outcome.
The thirty-three theorem declarations establish finite consequences over modeled assumptions and do not prove general DAG acyclicity, dependency truth, planner quality, or runtime refinement.
Both empirical transitions explicitly preserve no support-state change.
Extra search, context, retries, parallelism, or human repair can explain apparent plan quality while increasing total cost.
Fail-closed planning can look safe by dispatching nothing and suppress useful opportunity.
known_confounds: Reference decompositions and dependency labels can be incomplete, outcome-informed, culturally narrow, or non-unique.
Governed planners may receive more tokens, search, calls, tools, context, feedback, repair, or human labor than baselines.
Planner, dependency adjudicator, route qualifier, verifier, and outcome evaluator may share models, data, code, infrastructure, organizations, or incentives.
Discarded plans, blocked nodes, retries, replans, merge failures, timeouts, and residuals can be removed from denominators.
Critical-path estimates can hide coordination, verification, recovery, privacy, rights, and opportunity costs.
Non-dispatch can reduce observed harm while increasing false denial and missed help.
Short horizons can miss downstream effects, descendant invalidation, recovery incompleteness, and delayed harm.
A synthetic schema or finite theorem can be mistaken for an exercised planner mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: Every command, candidate, node, edge, assumption, observation, route, lifecycle, schedule, dispatch, merge, stop, recovery, replan, denominator, cost, and residual field has an exact producer, consumer, owner, valid case, rejecting mutation, and observed consequence.

A preregistered natural multi-planner campaign meets every joint coverage, dependency, feasibility, calibration, usefulness, unsafe-action, abstention, missed-help, recovery, cost, causal, replication, and transfer gate against strong matched comparators.

Each claimed mechanism has a predicted causal signature that disappears under matched ablation and cannot be explained by extra resources, hidden human repair, weak baselines, evaluator dependence, denominator changes, or non-dispatch.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact consumer, purpose, task, command, planner, model, observation set, dependency labels, route, scheduler, authority, rights, tools, budget, evaluator, threat, horizon, outcomes, costs, and residuals.

Separate plan validity, graph acyclicity, dependency truth, route eligibility, dispatch request, observed effect, artifact quality, useful outcome, safety, recovery, support movement, and live authority.

Unknown decomposition adequacy, dependency truth, feasibility, evaluator independence, external effects, delayed outcomes, causal attribution, replication, or transfer remains residual, blocked, narrowed, or refuted.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable ordinary or adversarial trace violates it or a required field lacks an exercised producer, consumer, owner, or consequence.

Refute a mechanism benefit when matched ablation preserves the joint outcome, the mechanism worsens the frontier, or its effect is explained by extra resources, human repair, weak baselines, denominator changes, or non-dispatch.

Refute useful, safe, optimal, superior, reproducible, or transferable planning language when a simpler comparator dominates, dependencies or observations are wrong, effects escape, recovery fails, or replication and transfer fail.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise intent, compilation, routing, labor, runtime, security, verification, resource, evidence, rights, or release owner supersedes the atom and every consumer migrates with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze and execute the natural multi-planner campaign with direct, single-pass, search, HTN/PDDL, workflow, human, learned-model, scheduler, and router baselines; hidden obligations and perturbations; independent dependency and outcome adjudication; identical information, authority, tools, and budgets; complete alternatives and costs; effect-tested stops and recovery; causal ablations; clean reproduction; and transfer.

non_claims: A complete plan record, acyclic graph, solver optimum, planner score, selected route, dispatch request, fixture, finite theorem, or no-change transition does not establish dependency truth, plan usefulness, safety, optimality, or production transfer.

The current seven valid records, twenty-six rejecting controls, two no-change transitions, and thirty-three theorem declarations establish only their exact record checks, finite branch consequences, and non-promotion boundaries.
No plan, job, authority, effect, support state, deployment, publication, AGI, or ASI claim changes without its separate owner and accepted transition.

planning-as-a-control-layer.core.strongest_counterclaim: Existing planners, workflow engines, schedulers, routers, and human review provide adequate control at lower complexity and cost.
planning-as-a-control-layer.core.falsifier: Any accepted plan violates a frozen obligation or bound, wrong dependencies or feasibility pass, or a strong simpler comparator dominates the joint frontier.
acceptance_criteria: Meet every preregistered natural coverage, dependency, usefulness, safety, cost, causal, replication, and transfer gate against strong comparators.

planning-as-a-control-layer.minimum.001.strongest_counterclaim: The present validators and proofs already implement the planning control layer.
planning-as-a-control-layer.minimum.001.falsifier: Any current artifact is narrated as planner, scheduler, natural-outcome, or support evidence beyond its exact record or finite route.
acceptance_criteria: Run actual planners on natural held-out workloads with matched baselines, independent adjudication, observed effects and costs, causal ablations, reproduction, and transfer.

planning-as-a-control-layer.beyond_sota..strongest_counterclaim: The full control plane compounds correlated errors and governance cost while simpler systems remain more useful.
planning-as-a-control-layer.beyond_sota..falsifier: Any joint gate fails, causal signatures disappear, a simpler baseline dominates, or independent replication and transfer fail.
acceptance_criteria: Complete the multi-comparator causal, adversarial, independently implemented, replicated, and transferred program and meet every joint gate.

planning-as-a-control-layer.invariant.00.strongest_counterclaim: Typed edge declarations and cycle checks are sufficient for bounded workflows.
planning-as-a-control-layer.invariant.00.falsifier: A dated natural audit finds declared dependency semantics and acyclicity predict every required ordering and omission without material error.
acceptance_criteria: Measure dependency precision, recall, provenance, falsifiers, and consequences against independent labels and counterexamples.

planning-as-a-control-layer.invariant.00.strongest_counterclaim: Downstream authority checks make planner-side dispatch gates redundant.
planning-as-a-control-layer.invariant.00.falsifier: Stale or missing dependency, context, route, authority, rights, resource, verifier, stop, or recovery state can request or cause a material effect.
acceptance_criteria: Inject every stale or missing predicate through real dispatch and observe denial before effect with measured false-denial cost.

planning-as-a-control-layer.invariant.00.strongest_counterclaim: Ordinary workflow versioning and rerun semantics preserve enough state for practical replanning.
planning-as-a-control-layer.invariant.00.falsifier: A replan widens authority, erases stops or alternatives, reruns unaffected work, loses costs or residuals, or silently changes the contract.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise live perturbations with typed dependency closure, observed effects, preserved history, recovery, and independent outcome adjudication.

planning-as-a-control-layer.invariant.00.strongest_counterclaim: Best-plan and completed-node reporting is enough when exclusions are documented.
planning-as-a-control-layer.invariant.00.falsifier: Any generated alternative, block, timeout, retry, replan, merge failure, denial, cost, or residual disappears without changing reported conclusions.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile generator emissions, plan versions, node states, route and dispatch requests, effects, costs, and residuals exactly.

planning-as-a-control-layer.invariant.00.strongest_counterclaim: Lower dispatch or shorter nominal critical path is a sufficient operational proxy for safer and cheaper planning.
planning-as-a-control-layer.invariant.00.falsifier: A prospectively matched study validly infers joint usefulness, safety, and cost from those proxies without outcome or opportunity measurements.
acceptance_criteria: Require nonzero useful and unsafe opportunity plus matched outcomes, missed help, latency, recovery, and total cost.

planning-as-a-control-layer.failure_mode.strongest_counterclaim: Acyclicity plus declared edge types makes the graph operationally trustworthy.
planning-as-a-control-layer.failure_mode.falsifier: Natural counterexample search finds no false, missing, stale, or circular semantic dependency that passes the checker.
acceptance_criteria: Test independently labeled false and missing edges, semantic cycles, stale observations, and alternative valid decompositions.

planning-as-a-control-layer.failure_mode.strongest_counterclaim: Capability scores and expected cost are adequate for planner route selection.
planning-as-a-control-layer.failure_mode.falsifier: Selected routes meet frozen adequacy, outcome, rights, latency, and total-cost gates across hidden tasks without independent qualification.
acceptance_criteria: Compare proposed and qualified routes under blinded natural outcomes and complete cost-quality ledgers.

planning-as-a-control-layer.failure_mode.strongest_counterclaim: Keeping only the latest plan is sufficient because superseded states are operationally irrelevant.
planning-as-a-control-layer.failure_mode.falsifier: Deleting prior plans, alternatives, assumptions, costs, and failed attempts never changes audit, recovery, or outcome conclusions.
acceptance_criteria: Recompute repair and outcome claims from full and latest-only histories under adversarial perturbations.

planning-as-a-control-layer.failure_mode.strongest_counterclaim: Planner-produced candidates rejected before scheduling add no useful information.
planning-as-a-control-layer.failure_mode.falsifier: A clean full-emission replay finds no material change in quality, cost, abstention, or failure estimates.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile and rescore all generated, malformed, rejected, blocked, timed-out, replanned, and residualized plans.

planning-as-a-control-layer.failure_mode.strongest_counterclaim: Conservative blocking is intrinsically safer even if useful throughput is not measured.
planning-as-a-control-layer.failure_mode.falsifier: Matched nonzero-opportunity trials show blocking improves the joint safety-usefulness frontier without missed-help or cost regressions.
acceptance_criteria: Measure useful throughput, unsafe dispatch, abstention, missed help, latency, and total governance cost together.

planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.plann.strongest_counterclaim: The declarations assume the modeled authority predicates and do not validate grants, inheritance, or enforcement.
planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.plann.falsifier: They are cited as proof that a real plan cannot widen authority or a forged or stale predicate passes.
acceptance_criteria: Refine against typed grants, command lineage, expiry, revocation, observed state, and executable effect gates.

planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.plann.strongest_counterclaim: A Boolean conjunction proves only its own declared policy and not semantic feasibility or runtime blocking.
planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.plann.falsifier: A semantically invalid record reaches dispatch or the theorem is cited as planner safety evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Connect typed semantic and authority producers to observed effect-bearing dispatch with negative controls.

planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.plann.strongest_counterclaim: Trusted Booleans and route priority do not establish graph completeness, dependency truth, or planner quality.
planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.plann.falsifier: A false or incomplete graph reaches admission or prose calls route coverage planning correctness.
acceptance_criteria: Bind typed graph objects, independent dependency labels, countermodels, executable producers, and runtime consequences.

planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.plann.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem trusts fixture summary bits and proves no scheduler policy, optimality, or deployed behavior.
planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.plann.falsifier: A malformed trace can set the bits or the theorem is cited as scheduler evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Connect typed traces to an independently implemented scheduler, observed dispatch, costs, outcomes, and perturbations.

planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.plann.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem restates synthetic delta fields and proves neither affected-subgraph correctness nor useful recovery.
planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.plann.falsifier: Incorrect repair scope or erased state satisfies the summary, or the theorem is cited as deployed replanning evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Connect typed dependency closure and observed feedback to live replans, effects, recovery, outcomes, and countermodels.

planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.planf.strongest_counterclaim: Increasing node indices encode only a chosen order and do not prove general acyclicity, dependency truth, or completeness.
planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.planf.falsifier: The theorem is cited as general DAG correctness or a false dependency graph satisfies the ordering predicate.
acceptance_criteria: Model typed finite graphs, reachability, cycles, semantic dependency validity, missing edges, and executable scheduling refinement.

planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.planf.strongest_counterclaim: The valid-outcome predicate assumes the desired escalation or residual policy and proves no verifier quality or runtime behavior.
planning-as-a-control-layer.formal.planf.falsifier: A failed quality predicate is silently completed in execution or the theorem is cited as verifier or recovery evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Bind independent quality evaluation to observed escalation, residual custody, retry cost, and terminal outcomes.

non_claims: This semantic review does not promote the chapter above argument or convert schema, fixture, theorem, scheduler trace, replan trace, no-change decision, or green validation into planner-quality, safety, usefulness, or production evidence.

This review does not establish a correct decomposition, true dependency graph, calibrated world model, adequate route, optimal schedule, useful replan, effect-complete recovery, independent evaluator, reproducible transfer, AGI, or ASI.
No external-human prepublication review is required or claimed, and no external publication or release is authorized by this packet.
